qa_pairs.question: When did the Mist cluster require a temporary shutdown?
qa_pairs.answer: The Mist cluster required a temporary shutdown for a few hours on January 6.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Rorqual compute cluster fully unavailable for maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: The Rorqual compute cluster was fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance on September 3, 2025, from 8:00 a.m. (EDT) until 5:00 p.m. (EDT).

qa_pairs.question: What was the status of Rorqual's compute nodes during the September 3, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: All compute nodes on Rorqual were offline during the September 3, 2025, outage.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs scheduled to complete on Rorqual after 8:00 a.m. (EDT) on September 3, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs scheduled to complete after 8:00 a.m. (EDT) on September 3, 2025, remained in the queue until service returned.

qa_pairs.question: Were Rorqual's network and storage systems available during the September 3, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: No, network and storage systems associated with Rorqual were unavailable during the September 3, 2025, outage.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems were not affected by the Rorqual outage on September 3, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Narval cluster and cloud instances on the Béluga and Juno platforms were not affected by the Rorqual outage on September 3, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: From what date did the Cedar cluster operate at 70% capacity?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting January 22, 2025, the Cedar cluster operated at approximately 70% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: How long was the Cedar cluster expected to operate at 70% capacity starting January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cluster was expected to operate at approximately 70% capacity until Fir was commissioned during the summer of 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the planned extended Cedar outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was scheduled to support critical data-centre power and cooling upgrades for a new system installation, as part of infrastructure renewal activities.

qa_pairs.question: What was the total duration of the Cedar outage that commenced on June 16, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar outage, originally planned for 11 days (June 16 to June 27, 2025), was extended to July 1, 2025, resulting in a total duration of 16 days.

qa_pairs.question: Was login node access available during the Cedar outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, login node access was unavailable for the entire outage period due to vendor data synchronization work.

qa_pairs.question: How were Cedar file systems accessible during the June/July 2025 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: File systems were accessible read-only via Globus only throughout the outage. During the week of June 16, all file systems had read-only access, which was limited to the old project file system during the week of June 23.

qa_pairs.question: What was the status of job submissions during the Cedar outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Job submissions were suspended during the outage; previously submitted jobs ran once the outage concluded.

qa_pairs.question: Did Cedar Cloud remain operational during the June/July 2025 Cedar outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Cedar Cloud remained operational, though brief interruptions occurred during the maintenance window.

qa_pairs.question: What unexpected event led to a system reset during the Cedar outage in June/July 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: An unexpected cooling tower failure over the weekend required a system reset to bring the cluster back online.

qa_pairs.question: What action was required from users for job scheduling during the Cedar outage in June/July 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No action was required, but users should have planned accordingly for job scheduling.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems were shut down for electrical maintenance from June 13 to June 16, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga and Narval compute nodes, as well as cloud instances in the Juno Cloud (non-High Availability zone), were shut down for electrical maintenance from 12:00 p.m. (noon EDT) on June 13 until 12:00 p.m. (noon EDT) on June 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Were Béluga cloud instances or Juno Cloud instances in the High Availability zone affected by the June 13-16, 2025, maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Béluga cloud instances and Juno cloud instances in the High Availability zone were not affected by these interruptions.

qa_pairs.question: Was storage accessible on Béluga and Narval during the June 13-16, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Béluga and Narval storage remained accessible via Globus and the login nodes of each cluster.

qa_pairs.question: When was the first scheduled electrical maintenance that affected Béluga and Narval compute nodes in June 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The first scheduled electrical maintenance that affected Béluga and Narval compute nodes was from 9:00 a.m. (EDT) on June 6 until 12:00 p.m. (noon) on June 10, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: How were cloud instances on Béluga Cloud and in the Juno Cloud HA zone affected by the June 6-10, 2025, maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: Cloud instances on Béluga Cloud and in the Juno Cloud HA zone experienced short access outages.

qa_pairs.question: How were storage systems on Béluga and Narval affected during the June 6-10, 2025, maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: Storage systems on Béluga and Narval remained accessible via Globus and login nodes but saw intermittent disruptions due to the work.
